column1: Good morning. I have an appointment for fingerprints.
column2: Buongiorno. Ho un appuntamento per le impronte digitali.

column1: I have the appointment letter, here.
I'm here for the residence permit.
column2: Sono qui per il permesso di soggiorno.

column1: Here's the passport and the receipts.
column2: Ecco il passaporto e le ricevute.

column1: Sorry, I don't speak Italian well.
Can you speak more slowly, please?
column2: Può parlare più lentamente, per favore?

column1: I didn't understand. Can you repeat?
Can you write it here, please?
When can I collect the permit?
How do I know when it's ready?
column2: Come faccio a sapere quando è pronto?

column1: Do I need to bring other documents?
Thanks very much, have a good day.
Documenti, prego. / Mi dia il passaporto.
column2: Documents, please. / Give me the passport. → Hand over the folder.
Come in / take a seat. → Sit.
Counter number 3. → Go to that counter.

column1: Metta il dito qui. / Sullo scanner.
column2: Put your finger here / on the scanner.

column1: Tutta la mano. / Solo il pollice.
Prema. / Più forte. / Non si muova.

column2: Look at the camera. Don't smile.

column1: Manca un documento. / Mancano le foto.
column2: A document is missing. / Photos missing.
Come back on... → Note the date; ask them to write it.
You'll get an SMS. → Watch your phone.
Date of birth? → Sono nato/a il ...
Where were you born? → Sono nato/a a ...
Where do you live? / Address? → Abito in via ...
Reason for the permit? → Per lavoro / studio / famiglia.
How do you spell it? → A come Ancona, B come Bologna...
Shall we play? / Play a set?

column1: Tocca a te servire. / Servi tu.
